fires_if: nausea; vomiting; diarrhea; orthostasis; constipation_with_low_fluid_intake; low_oral_intake; heat_exposure
why: Best-supported IV support for this stack is symptom-driven hydration because retatrutide’s incretin/glucagon axis has GI adverse-signal overlap and GLP-1 class labels warn about dehydration/renal risk.

why: Reasonable if reduced intake or B12-deficiency risk exists; not a generic energy or peptide-booster treatment.
evidence_grade: A for B12 risk triggers; C for general IV wellness

why: May support nutrition/recovery context relevant to GH/IGF-1 axis, but no direct evidence it enhances tesamorelin/ipamorelin outcomes.

iv_option: Niagen / NR IV or NAD+ IV

why: This aligns conceptually with MOTS-c mitochondrial axis, but direct MOTS-c + NAD/NR combination evidence is absent. NR/Niagen IV has preliminary tolerability evidence only.
